mnemonics: Same Name, Different Signature - Overloading is like having multiple tools with the same brand name (method name) but different shapes (parameter lists) for different jobs.
Vary Your Arguments - Overloading is all about changing the method's 'diet' (parameters), not its 'body type' (return type).
keyPoints: Overloading occurs when two or more methods in the same class have the same name but different parameter lists (type, number, or order).
Return type alone is not sufficient to overload a method; the parameter list MUST be different.
Overloaded methods are resolved at compile-time based on the reference type and the arguments passed.
commonMistakes: Attempting to overload a method by only changing its return type, which results in a compile-time error.
Confusing method overloading (compile-time polymorphism) with method overriding (runtime polymorphism).
